content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

August 31, 2004

Louis R. Calcagno
Monterey County
Board of Supervisors
Post Office Box 787
Castroville, CA 95012

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-04-173

Dear Mr. Calcagno:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the statement of
economic interests provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

QUESTION

Must you file an amended Form 700 because of your financial interest in a
nonprofit water company?

CONCLUSION

No. You do not have an investment in or business position with the water
company.

FACTS

You are a member of the Monterey County Board of Supervisors. You are a
director of the Moss Landing Mutual Water Company, a nonprofit mutual benefit
corporation. The Louis and Carol Calcagno Family Trust owns one share of the
corporation. In our telephone conversation on August 4, 2004, you said that you receive
no income from the corporation.

___________________________
¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. A-04-173
Page No. 2

ANALYSIS

As a county supervisor, you are required to disclose investments, interests in real
property, and income. (Section 87203.)

Section 82034 defines "investment" to mean "any financial interest in or security
issued by a business entity...." Section 82005 defines "business entity" to mean a for-
profit enterprise or organization. As a nonprofit mutual benefit corporation, the Moss
Landing Mutual Water Company is not a business entity as defined by the Act. Therefore
neither you nor the Louis and Carol Calcagno Family Trust have an investment interest in
the water company. (Elder Advice Letter, No. A-98-275.)

You are also required to report business positions. (Section 87209.) The term
"business position" refers to a business entity in which the designated employee is a
director, officer, partner, trustee, employee, or holds any position of management.
(Section 87209.) Since a nonprofit organization is not a business entity, the term
"business position" does not include a position held with a nonprofit organization.
(Martin Advice Letter, No. A-99-289.)

Please note that this advice is limited to the question of whether you must report
your interest in the Moss Landing Mutual Water Company, and does not constitute an
in-depth review of your statement of economic interests. You are responsible for the
overall accuracy and completeness of your statement as required by law.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: Hal Dasinger
Political Reform Consultant
